body.title: Web channel __identify__ auto-ping at boot counts as interaction #1
body.result: Fix was already in place (early return before _process() in _process_network_msg). Added missing test: 3 cases covering no interaction_count bump, preseed() called, source-text regression guard.
body.proposal: Igor wants to edit wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py (HIGH inertia). Plan: . Reason: HIGH inertia write requires human approval: wild_igor/igor/brainstem/core_patterns.py
body.description:
When Igor boots, the web channel (via utility_closet_client or web/server) emits an `__identify__:Akien` frame as handshake/auth. That frame is processed by the main loop as a turn — visible in the boot log as 'Interaction #1' with 'from=Akien; initial_load', triggering narrative_engine.run, tier2/tier3 reasoning, and a full turn counter increment. It is NOT a user message. It pollutes interaction counts, turn trace history, and (worst) makes the last-user-message window non-deterministic when an auditor is trying to check 'what did Akien actually say'. Fix: recognize `__identify__:` prefix in the web-channel inbound path and route it to an auth-handshake handler, not the interaction loop.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/web/server.py (or wherever web inbound hits main.py._process_network_msg), wild_igor/igor/main.py (inbound message router), tests/test_web_identify_handshake.py (new). **Design rules:** test-plan-or-why-not. **Scope boundary:** IN — detect `__identify__:<name>` prefix, route to auth, do not count as interaction, do not trigger NE/reasoning/turn-trace. OUT — redesigning web auth flow or the handshake protocol itself (just sort the router). **Test plan:** unit test inbound router with `__identify__:Akien` → assert no interaction counter bump, no turn-trace entry, auth side-effect recorded. Same router with regular message → assert full interaction path.
